reviews.review_msg: Great professor and genuinely a nice guy overall. This class was fun and engaging, with two 'big' assignments (one of them being a group project). Special shoutout to Sage, great TA!
Chris is a great prof. But the 201W course (curriculum and context) itself was dry.

Chris is one of the best profs I've had at SFU. He is so funny and does his best to make lectures entertaining despite its dry content. There are quite a few things to do (quizzes, 3 assignments, labs, tutorial) but the grading scheme is fair and if you put in the work you will succeed! Love Chris!!!!
Chris is one of the best profs for CMNS classes!! He's easy-going, straightforward, and his lectures are easy to follow. Would definitely recommend taking his classes.

Chris is one of the best profs for cmns! His lectures can be quite dry at times but his personality is silly lol. Graded by three easy assignments, quizzes that are simple (just re read slides), and writing exercises in person at the end of class. 10/10 recommend :D

One of the most detailed, clear and caring profs at the entire university. He actually wants you to succeed and even better, wants to help you do it. Do not expect a prof who throws out a guideline and calls it a day. He walks you through what you need to know so specifically that it makes it feel easy to succeed.

Great prof! If you attend lectures and study what is on the slides, you will do fine on the tests. Assignments are marked by your TA (or at least mine were), so how tough stuff is graded really depends on them. Overall, it was a very straightforward and relatively easy course.

Chris is one of my favourite CMNS prof! Super nice and genuinely cares for his students. This was such a fun class because we got to use some really cool equipments and actually take what we learn and apply it. Would definitely take his classes again!

Chris is a great professor who really cares about his students. He seemed very knowledgeable about what he taught and explained his concepts in lecture really well. For the quizzez, if you attend the lecture he usually hinted at what would come, and usually it would be there on the quiz.

He is such a great prof! It's clear he is passionate and wants his students to learn. He is willing to answer any and all questions and teaches the material in a fun way. I actually enjoyed the lectures even though the material can be dry.
Amazing prof. Very supportive and genuinely cares about his student's success.

Great prof! The course material for CMNS201w is pretty dry, but the prof engages the content in interesting ways and is very involved with the labs and tutorials. Easily approachable and down-to-earth professor.
Be aware of confusion. The class needs to be structured better.
HE IS AMAZING! Chris is so helpful and personable, he is there to help literally at any time. I can't say enough about this prof, you have to take a course with him!

He was totally understanding of COVID situations and made sure everyone gets bonus points etc. Would definitely take his course in the future if I can, and one of the most understanding prof Ive met in SFU. Lecture videos were short, but summarized the key points for each chapter.

Chris was one of the best professors I have had at SFU. CMNS 201 was my first Q class and I was very nervous, but Chris explained everything in a way that made total sense. He is very empathetic towards his students, and this was especially prevalent during the COVID-19 crisis, as he constantly gave us many opportunities to earn extra grades!!

Chris is such a sweet guy but the format for 226 seemed messy. It was a bit chaotic and disorganized. The TA was a migraine and we didn't get to learn as much Adobe Premiere as past classes did. It was very much of watching tutorials and learning on your own. Chris is almost too nice to the point where this class felt too lax and he just sat back.

Chris is a nice guy, but the format of 226 is a bit weird. Attendance is mandatory because there's always a workshop portion of the class and that has participation marks attached to it. All the readings are done in class, which is fine, and there are online discussions every week. It's work intensive, especially for the final group video project.
chris is soooooo nice, everything you need to know is on the lecture slides, don't bother with the textbook.

Chris is honestly so caring and such a sweet professor. He genuinely cares and he knows that Q credit classes tend to scare people, so he simplifies the course and makes it as easy as possible. As long as you go to lectures it's a relatively easy course, considering it's 4 credits. Can't say enough good things about this guy!

This semester, I was fortunate to have Mr. Jeschelnik as an instructor for two of my courses and a lab instructor for another one. One may think that having the same teacher for almost all of your classes can be boring however, it was quite the opposite.
I have been taking Chris' courses for three consecutive terms and I can easily state that these terms have been the most joyful times of my academic journey. He is one of a kind!

Let me begin this review by mentioning that I am taking a total of 3 courses this semester and Chris is the instructor of two and the teaching assistant for the third one. This term so far is the most fun semester of my entire academic journey and I owe it all to Mr. Jeschelnik.

Mr. Jeschelnik is the best professor I have ever met. Not only he will make sure that you do not fall behind with a genuine interest towards recognizing you and your effort. he will always, I repeat, always keep the lectures as hilarious as possible. The class allows the creativity to arise and with his help you can do wonders. Simply, the best!

The most caring and down to earth prof I had ever have. He cares about his students, and want every person in the class to learn and have a good experiences. One of the most underrated professor, I really wish he could get promoted and become better in future!

Chris is easily the most human and most helpful prof I've had in the CMNS dept. He takes boring brutally dull material and somehow makes it all quite palatable. I'm almost done at SFU, but if I took more courses I'd take anything he's teaching.

Chris is thee best! I was really worried about taking this class, but he broke everything down so well and explained as clear as possible, making this one of my absolute favourite courses :) Teach more upper levels, Chris!!

Chris was one of the best profs I've had at SFU. I honestly wasn't looking forward to this class at all and it ended up being one of my favourites in my whole degree because of his teaching methods. Chris is super easy-going, well-organized, and genuinely cares about his students. His lectures are informative, and sometimes kinda funny!ThanksChris!

Chris was an amazing Prof! I took this class in the Summer during intersession. He was one of the best Professors I have ever had! Went out of his way to make sure everyone knew what they were doing in the lab. Marking was straight forward, fair, and honestly easy. 3 assignments, 2 tests, and a final. Easy read for the textbook. TAKE IT WITH HIM!
LOOOVE HIM 3 I would take every course with him if I could! Probs one of my favourite proffs!

Chris is so enthusiastic and encouraging! His energy  is infectious and he really got me stoked on messing around with SPSS (even though I'm not that into computers).  I think he may be the best prof I've had so far at SFU!

Chris is a super kind proff. He's always wiling to help. He always seems to have a smile on his face; he's very personable. Since I took the course intersession, he was the professor and the lab instructor. He explains the material really well and makes sure everyone in the class understands. In labs, we practiced what he taught in lecture. EASY!!!
